mechanic.description: If a player plays a card with value 10, they must pause and the next turn is skipped for the other player (they lose their next play), while the scoring of the 10 still applies normally.
mechanic.python_code:
import numpy as np

def wild_pause_action(game_state: dict) -> dict:
    # Initialize custom state
    cs = game_state.setdefault('custom_state', {})
    cs.setdefault('skip_opponent_once', False)

    # If it is currently the opponent's turn and they are marked to be skipped, skip their play.
    current_player = game_state.get('current_player', 1)
    other_player = 2 if current_player == 1 else 1

    if cs.get('skip_opponent_once', False) and game_state.get('turn', 0) is not None:
        # Skip exactly one opponent turn by advancing turn control to the same player next.
        # We model this by clearing the flag and granting an extra turn to the player whose turn it was meant to be skipped.
        cs['skip_opponent_once'] = False
        game_state['extra_turn'] = True
        # current_player remains the same (since extra_turn keeps them next)
        return game_state

    # Apply new effect only when a 10 was just played.
    last_played = game_state.get('last_played', None)
    if last_played == 10:
        # Mark that the other player will have their very next turn skipped.
        cs['skip_opponent_once'] = True
        game_state['extra_turn'] = False

    return game_state
mechanic.justification: This adds a clear, single-card decision point that creates tactical tempo swings without directly stacking scores or relying on complex resources.

parent_summary: Baseline game without the new mechanic.
